patches.find: Note also that the SIGN of the asymmetry (NH > SH) is independently predicted by standard climate science from differential land-mass distribution and aerosol forcing — so getting the direction right is non-discriminating evidence for the dome model.
patches.replace: Defender pivot anticipated. A dome advocate may concede the geometry produces a real center-rim flux gradient and pivot to: "we predict the SIGN (NH > SH); the 90–270 W/m² magnitude is your imputation, not ours; sign-match is meaningful confirmation." Three counters preempt this. (1) The SIGN of the asymmetry (NH > SH) is independently predicted by standard climate science from differential land-mass distribution (~39% NH vs ~19% SH), albedo asymmetry, and time-varying aerosol/cloud forcing — and the actual Loeb et al. 2025 abstract attributes the trend specifically to "changes in hemispheric differences in aerosol-radiation interactions, surface albedo, and water vapor changes." A sign-match against an independent baseline that gets the same sign through different mechanisms is non-discriminating. (2) The dome cites a specific value (+0.34 W/m²/decade) as confirmation, not "any positive NH-SH difference." Sign-only predictions cannot single out a particular number as confirmation; the +0.34 coincidence is post-hoc adoption from CERES literature, not a derivation. (3) Disclaiming the inverse-cube integral that follows from the dome’s own h=5,733 km geometry leaves the model with no quantitative prediction at all — a sign-only model with a 50/50 NH > SH-vs-SH > NH prior is equivalent to a coin flip for falsification. Either the dome predicts a magnitude (and is wrong by ~250–800×), or it predicts only sign (and any positive value would have confirmed, making the +0.34 coincidence content-free).
patches.rationale: EXP-296 defense neutralization: ISS-1917 defense_survives=3. Expands the single trailing sign-non-discrimination sentence into a three-counter Defender pivot anticipated paragraph addressing both (a) sign-non-discrimination and (b) the magnitude-disclaim retreat. Includes Loeb et al. 2025 mechanism citation.
EXP-300-P1: Propagate decider Polaris luminosity patch (1,260 -> 2,400 L☉) to finding field. Detail_evidence was already updated; finding was missed.
EXP-300-P2: Propagate decider Polaris luminosity patch to tldr_evidence. Detail_evidence already updated; tldr was missed.

patches.find: The two commitments cannot both be held.
patches.replace: The dome’s ~30,000 km maximum spatial extent and its citation of 1–100 kpc rotation curves as confirmation cannot both be held.
patches.rationale: WIN-052.c3 hole #2 (minor): deictic-ambiguity fix per curmudgeon recommendation. Replaces 'The two commitments' with explicit antecedents.
